title: cyminosma pedunculata
intro: Cyminosma pedunculata, also known as the 'Kawakawa', is a native New Zealand plant belonging to the family Apiaceae. It has been used for centuries in traditional Māori medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Kawakawa plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Kawakawa is often prepared as a tea or infused oil for its medicinal uses, and can also be found in various herbal preparations, including capsules and topical creams.
